do: I do my homework every evening.
She does the dishes after dinner.
He has done the task already.
We are doing our best now.
say: He has said too much already.
We are saying the same thing now.
go: I go to work by bus.
He has gone to the store.
We are going to the station now.
get: I get good ideas at night.
She gets email from her boss.
They got a new table yesterday.
make: He has made a big mistake.
know: He has known her for years.
think: I think about my future often.
She thinks the idea is good.
They thought about the trip yesterday.
He has thought about that before.
We are thinking about dinner now.
take: I take the bus every morning.
She takes her laptop to work.
They took the wrong train yesterday.
He has taken my seat again.
see: I see the mountains from here.
She sees her friends every weekend.
They saw a good film yesterday.
He has seen that movie before.
We are seeing a doctor tomorrow.
come: She comes to class on time.
We are coming to the party.
want: I want a cup of tea.
He has wanted this for years.
look: I look at the screen all day.
They looked for the key yesterday.
We are looking at the map now.
use: I use this notebook every day.
She uses her phone for work.
They used the back door yesterday.
He has used all the paper already.
We are using the new system now.
find: I find this book very useful.
He has found a better solution.
We are finding new clients now.
give: I give my plants water every morning.
They gave us a ride yesterday.
He has given me the file already.
We are giving the children snacks now.
tell: I tell my son a story every night.
They told us the news yesterday.
He has told that joke before.
We are telling the driver our address now.
work: I work in an office downtown.
She works at a small hospital.
He has worked here for years.
We are working on a new project now.
call: I call my mother every Sunday.
She calls a taxi after work.
We are calling the hotel now.
